Microsoft Copilot Quick-Reference

A consolidated walkthrough covering the new Copilot interface, image & itinerary creation, research, Excel data analysis, email safety, personalisation, and the eight power prompts that transform everyday productivity.

📖 Sources: Copilot 101 · Data Analysis Essentials · 8 Must-Use Prompts
🎯 Format: Topics → Learning Objectives → Steps
⏱️ Reading time: ~15 min

How to use this guide

Every section is a stand-alone topic broken into three blocks: 🎯 Topics (what’s covered), ✅ Learning Objectives (what you’ll be able to do), and 📝 Steps (how to do it, with the exact prompts to use).

Prompt boxes are styled in dark monospace so you can copy them straight into Copilot. The reference table at the bottom shows which features need a paid Microsoft 365 Copilot licence and which work on the free tier.

📑 Contents

1 Getting Started with Copilot 2 Creative Outputs — Itineraries & Images 3 Research & Information Retrieval 4 Document & PDF Intelligence 5 Excel & Data Analysis Essentials 6 Goal-Oriented Email Drafting 7 Personalisation & Memory 8 Daily Productivity Power Prompts ★ Free vs Paid Reference
1

Getting Started with Copilot

The new Microsoft 365 Copilot experience, Edge integration, and the interface essentials.

Topics
  • The new Microsoft 365 Copilot home page (replacing office.com)
  • Launching Copilot inside Microsoft Edge
  • Anatomy of the Copilot interface and its key controls
Learning Objectives
  • Recognise the new Microsoft 365 Copilot environment as the replacement for office.com
  • Locate and launch Copilot directly from the Microsoft Edge browser
  • Identify the key components of the Copilot interface and what each one does
Steps
  1. Open Copilot from Microsoft Edge
    Open Microsoft Edge and click the Copilot icon in the sidebar to expand the chat window. Sign in with your Microsoft account.
  2. Learn the interface controls
    • Microsoft Entra Data Protection indicator — confirms your data is secured.
    • Start a New Fresh Chat — begin a new conversation at any time.
    • Pre-made Prompt Templates — e.g. “Gauge the temperature”, “Show me the result”, “Get instructions”.
    • Message Input Area — type your own custom prompts.
    • View Prompts — access your saved prompts for quick reuse.
  3. Access chat history and other features
    Open the side menu to revisit previous chats, manage saved prompts, and switch to advanced modes like Think Deeper.
  4. Try a template prompt before writing your own
    Templates are the fastest way to see what Copilot can do without typing a full instruction from scratch.
2

Creative Outputs — Itineraries & Images

Turn descriptive prompts into trip plans and photoreal images.

Topics
  • Generating detailed travel itineraries
  • Creating images for work or commercial use
  • The link between prompt detail and output quality
Learning Objectives
  • Write descriptive prompts that produce high-quality, usable itineraries
  • Generate professional, photorealistic images on demand
  • Iterate on outputs by refining a single element of the prompt
Steps
  1. Write a detailed itinerary prompt
    Cover trip purpose, travellers, destination, season, activities, transportation, and budget.
    Create for me a 10 day itinerary for an autumn Tokyo trip that involves 2 pax, husband and wife. I am looking for a fun trip, include places where you can have a casual hike, shopping, good and affordable restaurants, some amusement parks, include transportation direction to these places, include cost of these places and how much budget should I prepare.
  2. Refine with follow-up prompts
    Ask Copilot to swap days, lower the budget, or replace specific activities — it keeps the context from your first prompt.
  3. Generate an image with subject + style + mood
    For images, describe the subject, lighting, background, and overall mood.
    Create an image of a box of cookies. The box is blue in color. The image stands out and hyper photorealistic, as if it has been shot by a professional photographer. Add some crumbs around the box. Add a rustic wooden table as the background. Make the cookies look freshly baked. Set the theme to a cookie bakery.
  4. Iterate one element at a time
    Change one variable per follow-up (e.g. “make the box red instead”) to stay in control of the result.
3

Research & Information Retrieval

Web search, Think Deeper mode, right-click contextual lookup, and article summarisation.

Topics
  • Web search via Copilot
  • Think Deeper mode for complex questions
  • Right-click contextual menu in Edge
  • Article summarisation
Learning Objectives
  • Use Copilot as a fast research assistant for everyday questions
  • Decide when to switch from standard mode to Think Deeper
  • Use right-click highlighting to look up terms inline while browsing
  • Convert long articles into scannable key-points
Steps
  1. Ask an open question
    Treat Copilot like a knowledgeable colleague.
    What are some of the good stretches I can do during office hour which will help after seating for long hours?
  2. Switch to Think Deeper for complex topics
    Click the dropdown and choose Think Deeper. Expect about a 40-second wait in exchange for a more thorough, reasoned answer. Available on personal Microsoft accounts.
  3. Use the right-click menu while browsing
    Highlight any word or passage on a webpage, right-click, and pick a Copilot option for an instant definition, explanation, or summary.
  4. Summarise long articles
    • Open the Copilot window on the article you’re reading.
    • Choose Create A Summary.
    • Review the keypoints instead of reading end-to-end.
4

Document & PDF Intelligence

Chat with PDFs in Edge and convert long Word docs into team-ready emails.

Topics
  • Chatting with PDF documents in Microsoft Edge
  • Converting long Word documents into ready-to-send team emails
Learning Objectives
  • Extract answers from long PDFs without reading them end-to-end
  • Turn a long Word document into a concise email draft in a single prompt
Steps
  1. Open the PDF in Microsoft Edge
    Edge’s built-in PDF reader is fully integrated with Copilot.
  2. Click “Ask Copilot” and chat with the document
    Ask questions directly. Copilot pulls answers from the PDF content.
    What is this document about?
  3. Turn a Word doc into a team email
    Open the Word Copilot sidebar on a long document and run this prompt. Copy the result straight into Outlook.
    Extract the key messages from this document, and format the findings in the form of an email that I can send to the team.
5

Excel & Data Analysis Essentials

Formula help, supported file formats, and plain-English analysis in the Excel sidebar.

Topics
  • Excel formula lookup and quick tutorials
  • Supported file formats and size limits
  • What Copilot can do with your data
  • Plain-English business analysis in the Excel sidebar
Learning Objectives
  • Get on-demand Excel help (formulas, steps, tutorials) by chatting with Copilot
  • Prepare a dataset so Copilot can analyse it cleanly
  • Generate insights, charts, and formulas from raw data
  • Frame business questions in plain English to get useful answers
📥
Download the sample file before attempting the activity. Click here to download the sample Excel file — you'll upload this to Copilot in the steps below.
Steps
  1. Ask conversational Excel questions
    Skip Googling — just ask Copilot in plain English.
    I am new to Excel. How do I remove duplicates from column A?
  2. Prepare your file before uploading
    • Supported formats: Excel (.xlsx, .xls), CSV, TSV, text-based PDF
    • Maximum file size: 512 MB
    • Use clear column headers and remove blank rows inside the data range
  3. Start with a broad insight prompt
    Upload the file in chat and ask for a top-level read.
    Give me insights to this excel file
  4. Drill into specific questions
    Once Copilot has the data, you can keep asking.
    Which region has the highest growth rate?
    Create a bar chart showing Q1–Q4 revenue by region
    Generate an Excel formula to calculate growth rate between quarters
    Based on this data, what should our growth priorities be?
  5. Run the full plain-English analysis prompt in the Excel sidebar
    This single prompt produces trends, anomalies, and business-language insights in seconds.
    Review this data and help me understand what it is telling me. Identify any trends, patterns, or unusual values. Explain the insights in real business terms without using technical jargon, and suggest visualizations that could help improve how we see this data.
💡
Pro tips for better data analysis: use clear column headers · clean the data first (no blank rows inside the range) · ask specific questions like “what’s our churn trend?” rather than “analyse this” · request visualisations explicitly (“create a bar chart of X by Y”) · ask Copilot for the formula too, not just the answer.

📊 Summarise patterns

Describe what the data is showing in plain language.

🔢 Statistics on demand

Averages, sums, distributions, totals — ask and answer.

📈 Generate charts

Bar, pie, line — specify the type and the data columns.

🧮 Explain formulas

Copilot writes the formula and explains how it works.

🔍 Spot trends & outliers

Surface anomalies and concentration risks you might miss.

❓ Answer specific questions

Treat your spreadsheet like a chat partner.

6

Goal-Oriented Email Drafting

Draft targeted replies in Outlook by telling Copilot exactly what the email should achieve.

Topics
  • Drafting goal-oriented replies in the Outlook Copilot sidebar
Learning Objectives
  • Generate Outlook replies that achieve a specific goal in one pass
  • Use bracketed placeholders to control tone, intent, and required information
Steps
  1. Open the email you need to reply to in Outlook
    Keep the message visible on screen so Copilot can read it directly.
  2. Open the Outlook Copilot sidebar
    The Copilot icon sits in the Outlook ribbon. Open the sidebar before running the prompt.
  3. Run the goal-oriented reply prompt
    Fill in the bracketed sections with your specific goal and the details that must appear in the reply.
    Look at the visible email on my screen and draft a reply. [State exactly what you want the email to achieve / what the goal is], and ensure the reply includes [specify the exact details or points to include].
  4. Review, tweak, and send
    Read the draft for tone and accuracy. Ask Copilot to “make it shorter”, “add a thank-you line”, or “sound more formal” if needed.
7

Personalisation & Memory

Teach Copilot your communication and writing style once, then benefit forever.

Topics
  • Using Copilot Memory to remember your preferred style
Learning Objectives
  • Use Copilot’s memory feature so future drafts and summaries match your voice
Steps
  1. Open Copilot chat
    Use any Copilot surface (web, Edge, or M365 app).
  2. Run the personalisation prompt
    Copilot will ask you five questions and save your answers to memory.
    Please ask me five questions to get to know me better, such as my preferred communication and writing style. Once I answer, save this information to your memory to ensure all future conversations and responses are more relevant to how I work.
  3. Answer honestly
    The more accurate your answers, the better the personalisation.
  4. Optional: go deeper with 10 questions
    Re-run the prompt asking for 10 questions instead of 5 for richer personalisation.
  5. Watch future outputs match your voice
    Emails, summaries, and drafts will reflect your stated tone. Works on both Free & Paid Copilot.
8

Daily Productivity Power Prompts

Four prompts that cut through email + Teams noise across your whole Microsoft 365 tenant.

Topics
  • Contextual file searching across your Microsoft 365 tenant
  • Daily task triage from Outlook + Teams
  • Meeting action-item retrieval from transcripts
  • Project status catch-up across email, Teams, and meetings
Learning Objectives
  • Cut through digital noise to find files, tasks, decisions, and actions in seconds
  • Prepare for client or management updates without manually digging through inbox history
  • Identify which power prompts require a paid Copilot licence
Steps
  1. Find a file
    In Copilot Chat, surface relevant files with a reason why each one matches.
    Find the file related to [insert topic/project]. Along with finding it, list out exactly why this file could be helpful to me based on its contents.
  2. Triage your day
    Use Copilot Chat to scan emails + Teams and tell you what needs your attention today.
    Review my emails and Teams messages to identify where my attention is needed right now. Come back with a list of points and tasks I need to follow up on today.
  3. Pull meeting action items
    Never miss an action assigned to you in a meeting again.
    Look at my meeting history and meeting transcriptions from the past week. Summarize what was discussed, note any key decisions made, and list all specific actions that involve me.
  4. Catch up on a project
    Get an executive summary ready for your next client or management update.
    I need to get back up to speed on [insert project/opportunity name]. Please review my past emails, Teams conversations, and meetings, and generate a project status update ready for my next client meeting.
⚠️
Paid licence required. These four power prompts work only with a paid Microsoft 365 Copilot licence because they read your tenant’s emails, files, Teams chats, and meeting transcripts. The personalisation, image, itinerary, Excel-upload, and email-scam prompts in the earlier sections work on the free tier.

★ Quick Reference — Free vs Paid Copilot

Capability Free Copilot Paid M365 Copilot
Web chat, image generation, web research✓✓
Article & PDF summarisation in Edge✓✓
Think Deeper mode (personal account)✓✓
Copilot Memory (personalisation prompt)✓✓
Data analysis on uploaded Excel / CSV files✓✓
Excel / Word / Outlook in-app sidebars—✓
Reads tenant emails, Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint—✓
Tenant file search, meeting transcript retrieval, project catch-up—✓
